title: Daily Brief and Digest for Consulting Firms
metaDescription: A done-for-you daily brief and digest for consulting firms. Wake up to a single morning summary of leads, client work, money, and meetings.
excerpt: Replace the scramble of tabs and inboxes with one quiet morning brief. Your consulting firm starts each day knowing what matters and what to do next.

sections.intro:
If you run a consulting firm, most mornings start with the same friction. You open the inbox, scan the CRM, check the calendar, glance at invoices, then try to remember what a senior associate said about a client late on Friday. By the time you have a sense of the day, you have lost an hour and a bit of your patience. The Daily Brief and Digest is a done-for-you workflow that ends that scramble. Before you open your laptop, a single brief is waiting in your inbox. It tells you what is new, what is at risk, who needs a reply, what is on the calendar, and what was promised yesterday. It is written in your voice, scoped to your firm, and it runs whether you ask for it or not.

This is the least AI necessary to give a partner or principal a calm starting point every morning. No dashboards to log into. No notifications to chase. One brief, on a schedule, every working day.
sections.fourCFit:
The Daily Brief and Digest is a Capability that lives inside the Cadence layer of your AI Operating System. Cadence is what makes the AIOS run on its own, on a schedule, without being asked.

  • Context gives the brief its voice and priorities, so it knows what counts as a hot lead, a stalled proposal, or a senior client.
  • Connections let it read live data from your inbox, CRM, calendar, billing, and project docs.
  • Capabilities assemble and write the brief in plain language.
  • Cadence delivers it at the same time every morning, plus an optional end of day digest.

It pairs naturally with the free 60-Second Lead Engine, which answers new leads in under 60 seconds in your voice. The brief then tells you which of those leads actually moved overnight.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
The artifact is a short, scannable email or document delivered before your working day starts. It is structured and predictable, so you can read it in two minutes.

  • A morning brief delivered by 7:00 in your local time zone, Monday to Friday.
  • Sections for new leads, replies needing your attention, proposals out for signature, client deliverables due today, meetings with prep notes, and invoices that are unpaid or about to age.
  • A short "what changed since yesterday" block, so you do not have to remember.
  • A flagged risks section for client engagements that have gone quiet or slipped past a promised date.
  • Optional end of day digest at 18:00 with what closed, what moved, and what is open for tomorrow.
  • SLA: brief lands by 7:00 on at least 95 percent of business days. If a source system is down, the brief still ships with the rest of the data and a note about what is missing.
sections.howWeInstall:
We install the Daily Brief and Digest in three phases. You own the system at every step.

  1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes). We walk through your firm, your tools, and the decisions you make each morning. We agree on the sections that belong in your brief and what counts as a priority. Run the audit.
  2. Install (10 business days). We connect your inbox, CRM, calendar, billing, and project docs as read sources. We draft the brief template in your voice, run it in a quiet mode for several days, and tune what is included and what is filtered out. You approve the final format.
  3. Operate (ongoing). The brief runs every working day. We add one new automation per week to the AIOS, often refining how the brief detects risk, summarizes meetings, or rolls up partner workloads. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
• Roughly 30 to 60 minutes back per partner, per working day, from not assembling the morning picture by hand.
  • Faster response to client and lead emails, because nothing sits unseen for a day.
  • Fewer missed follow-ups on proposals and unpaid invoices, often the biggest hidden cost in a consulting firm.
  • Better Monday mornings and shorter internal stand-ups, because everyone is already oriented.
  • Our standing guarantee applies: across your AIOS, we help you recover 40 or more hours a month, or we keep working free until you do.
sections.faq:
Will the brief read confidential client emails?

It reads only what you connect, and only at the level of access you grant. Sensitive matters can be excluded by client, folder, or label. Your firm owns the system and the data, and you can revoke access at any time.

Can each partner get their own version?

Yes. We can deliver a tailored brief per partner or per practice area, with shared sections for firm-wide items like new leads and unpaid invoices. Junior staff can receive a lighter digest scoped to their accounts.

What if we already use a project or CRM dashboard?

The brief is meant to sit on top of the tools you already run on, not replace them. It is tool-agnostic, reads from your existing systems, and gives you a single morning summary so you do not have to open five tabs before coffee.
